title: lithocarpus pachyphyllus
intro: Lithocarpus pachyphyllus, also known as the Bur Oak, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Lithocarpus pachyphyllus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Lithocarpus pachyphyllus can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be administered orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
